fleet_evolution.note: Truck IDs appear to be year-of-truck (2013, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2019). New units '55' and '07' appeared starting Q1 2026 — fleet expanded.

fuel_vendors.note: Q2 2025 used TA + Pilot + EDS. Q3 2025 dropped TA, added Relay. Sheet header has 'Realy' typo in original.

anomalies_and_patterns: FLEET CHANGED Q1 2026: added 3 trucks (2016, '55', '07'). MPG may shift as new units enter.
FUEL VENDOR SWITCH Q3 2025: TA replaced with Relay. Verify per-vendor receipts are kept for audit.
Sheet headers in Q1/Q2 2026 say 'IFTA Q4 2025' — copy-paste typo by David in his template. Cosmetic.
California is the largest state by miles in every quarter (largest rate × largest miles). Largest tax contributor.
Heavy reliance on AZ/NV/NM/WY for fuel purchases — these tend to produce CREDIT lines on the return.
Surcharge states KY and VA appear sporadically — make sure surcharge lines are added when miles are present.

raw_inputs_typically_sent: Excel workbook with per-truck-per-state mileage (David's IFTA 2025 ACTIVED.xlsx template)
Fuel-card transaction PDF (Comdata-style 'IFTA Report' with per-purchase detail)
Per-state aggregated fuel CSV (Truck #, merchant_state, tax_paid, fuel_volume)
filing_workflow.base_state_portal: Kentucky DOR — David submits via KY's e-file system.

client_relationship: PRIMARY CLIENT — the user files David's IFTA returns on his behalf.

base_state.portal_hint: Files at https://drive.ky.gov/motor-carriers/ (Kentucky Transportation Cabinet) or via KY DOR e-file. Confirm the exact URL David uses.
notes_for_kentucky_filers: KY is David's base state. His IFTA license + decals issued by KY.
He has KY miles EVERY quarter (KY in 'always_visited' list) — Kentucky Surcharge line MUST appear on every return.
If any of his trucks are >59,999 lbs declared gross weight, KYU (Kentucky Weight Distance Tax) is filed SEPARATELY from IFTA, on its own quarterly schedule.
Penalty for late filing: greater of $50 or 10% of net tax due (IFTA standard).

per_quarter_filing_checklist: Confirm KY surcharge line is present (David has KY miles every quarter).
Verify Oregon shows $0 IFTA rate; Oregon WMT filed separately with ODOT.
Check VA surcharge if he has Virginia miles this quarter.
Verify all fleet trucks are on his IFTA decal list — fleet expanded in Q1 2026 (added 55, 07).
Cross-check fuel-vendor totals (TA/Pilot/EDS or Pilot/Relay/EDS depending on quarter) match the fuel-card PDF.
Confirm fleet MPG is in 6.4–7.0 historical band — anything below 5.5 or above 7.5 needs investigation.
Total miles should be 110K–135K for a full quarter — outliers indicate possible data gaps.
narrative_for_agent: Active multi-truck carrier, Kentucky base state, files via KY DOR. Fleet grew from 4 trucks (2013/2016/2017/2019) in Q1 2025 to 7 trucks in Q1 2026 (added 2015, 2016 returned, '55', '07'). Fuel vendors: TA+Pilot+EDS through Q2 2025, then Pilot+Relay+EDS from Q3 2025 onward. Heavy CA/AZ/NV/WY operator — California is the largest tax contributor every quarter. Historical MPG 6.45–6.94 (mean 6.77), miles 110K–133K per quarter. KY surcharge line applies EVERY quarter — David always has KY miles. KYU (Kentucky Weight Distance Tax) is a SEPARATE filing if any truck has declared gross weight > 59,999 lbs.
